agentTypes.label: Smart Agent
agentTypes.description: Uses the full workspace context: AGENTS.md, ROLE.md, SOUL.md, IDENTITY.md, USER.md, TOOLS.md, HEARTBEAT.md, MEMORY.md, and recent daily memory.
agentTypes.recommendedFor: assistant, CEO, CTO, PM, BA, and other strategic or generalist roles

agentTypes.label: Worker Agent
agentTypes.description: Uses only ROLE.md plus memory files. PROJECT.md can still be used when a project is attached.
agentTypes.recommendedFor: developers, QA, designers, and implementation-focused specialists

agentTypes.label: CLI Agent
agentTypes.description: Does not load workspace markdown files. It only uses PROJECT.md when project context is provided.
agentTypes.recommendedFor: bare CLI runners and low-context coding agents

roles.label: Assistant
roles.summary: High-context generalist who keeps work moving, tracks details, and helps the human think clearly.

roles.label: CEO
roles.summary: Company-level decision maker focused on strategy, priorities, risk, and business outcomes.

roles.label: CTO
roles.summary: Technical strategist focused on architecture, platform direction, execution tradeoffs, and engineering leverage.

roles.label: Product Manager
roles.summary: Owns product clarity, scope, prioritization, and outcome-driven delivery.

roles.label: Project Manager
roles.summary: Drives timelines, dependencies, status tracking, execution rhythm, and stakeholder alignment.

roles.label: Business Analyst
roles.summary: Translates ambiguous business needs into concrete requirements, process changes, and decision-ready analysis.

roles.label: Engineering Manager
roles.summary: Balances delivery, team health, staffing, execution process, and technical quality.

roles.label: Solutions Architect
roles.summary: Designs end-to-end systems and integrations with strong attention to tradeoffs and long-term maintainability.

roles.label: Tech Lead
roles.summary: Guides implementation quality, technical direction, decomposition, and code-level execution.

roles.label: Frontend Developer
roles.summary: Builds user-facing interfaces with strong attention to interaction quality, accessibility, and maintainable UI architecture.

roles.label: Backend Developer
roles.summary: Builds server-side systems, APIs, data flows, and operationally sound backend services.

roles.label: Full Stack Developer
roles.summary: Moves across frontend, backend, and integration layers to deliver complete features.

roles.label: Mobile Developer
roles.summary: Builds native or cross-platform mobile experiences with attention to performance, UX, and release quality.

roles.label: DevOps Engineer
roles.summary: Improves delivery pipelines, infra automation, reliability, observability, and deployment safety.

roles.label: QA Engineer
roles.summary: Finds risk, validates behavior, designs test coverage, and protects release quality.

roles.label: UI/UX Designer
roles.summary: Shapes flows, layouts, interaction patterns, and visual systems around user goals.

roles.label: Data Engineer
roles.summary: Builds reliable data pipelines, models, storage patterns, and analytics foundations.

roles.label: Machine Learning Engineer
roles.summary: Builds ML systems, evaluation loops, data preparation, and production model integrations.

roles.label: Security Engineer
roles.summary: Reduces security risk through threat modeling, hardening, review, and secure implementation guidance.

roles.label: Technical Writer
roles.summary: Turns complex systems and workflows into clear documentation, release notes, and decision records.

roles.label: Developer Relations
roles.summary: Bridges product, engineering, and developer audiences through examples, messaging, and ecosystem feedback.

roles.label: Support Engineer
roles.summary: Diagnoses customer issues, narrows root causes, and communicates fixes or workarounds clearly.
